id.description: Unique identifier for the Village entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the village, including its history, culture, and economy.

welcomeMessage.description: The welcome message displayed to the user.

primaryColor.description: The primary color of the village theme.

backgroundColor.description: The background color of the village theme.

accentColor.description: The accent color of the village theme.

font.description: The font used for the village theme.

definition.description: Stores the information for the village.  All data related to the village (name, description, welcome message, theme colors, font) is stored within this single document for easy retrieval.  There are no authorization independence concerns, as there are no subcollections.

firestore.reasoning:
The database structure is designed to store information about a single village. It prioritizes simplicity and ease of management. Since the application focuses on presenting information about one village, a single document is used to store all relevant data. This approach avoids the need for complex queries or relationships. By storing all the data in one document, retrieval is efficient and straightforward.

This structure inherently achieves Authorization Independence, as there are no subcollections or parent-child relationships that require `get()` calls in security rules. The single document contains all the data necessary for rendering the UI, eliminating the need to fetch related data. Since all data is contained within a single document, list operations are not applicable in this case.

The security rules for this configuration will simply validate that the authenticated user has the appropriate role to read/write to the village document. The simplicity of this design supports ease of debugging and maintenance.
